message:
test(self-healing): Add comprehensive tests for SelfHealingConfig and cooldown logic

Adds extensive test coverage for the self-healing configuration system that
allows hooks to auto-start the daemon and vice versa (bd-59kg).

**rch-common/src/types.rs - SelfHealingConfig tests:**
- `test_self_healing_config_defaults`: Validates default values
  (hook_starts_daemon=true, daemon_installs_hooks=true,
   auto_start_cooldown_secs=30, auto_start_timeout_secs=3)
- `test_self_healing_config_serde_full`: Full JSON round-trip serialization
- `test_self_healing_config_serde_partial_uses_defaults`: Partial JSON/TOML
  correctly fills in missing fields with defaults
- `test_self_healing_config_toml_with_alias`: Tests `daemon_start_timeout`
  alias for `auto_start_timeout_secs`

**rch/src/hook.rs - Cooldown integration tests:**
- `test_autostart_error_cooldown_active_variant`: Validates error variant
  structure and Debug formatting
- `test_cooldown_logic_simulation`: End-to-end simulation of cooldown
  behavior without touching real state files

**Note on test safety:**
Tests avoid direct env var manipulation (unsafe in Rust 2024 edition due to
data races in parallel tests). Environment override behavior is tested
through the config layer in rch/src/config.rs instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
